topics.title: Mod root layout and loadable folders
topics.body: A HOI4 mod is a mirror of selected game folders. Generated content should be placed under game-readable directories such as common, events, history, interface, gfx, map, music, sound, and localisation. A batch tool must treat paths as relative to the mod root and reject absolute paths or traversal.
relationships: Descriptor files advertise metadata; replace_path changes load semantics; localisation keys must match script IDs.
validation: Reject paths outside the mod root.
Keep generated script and localisation in separate files.

topics.title: descriptor.mod and launcher metadata
topics.body: The descriptor controls launcher metadata, supported version, tags, dependencies, and optional path replacement. Generators should not add replace_path unless the user explicitly wants to replace an entire vanilla folder, because it can hide vanilla content.
relationships: replace_path affects every generated file in the replaced folder.
supported_version relates to latest-update resources.
validation: Do not infer Steam workshop metadata unless provided.

topics.title: Load order, replace_path, and file precedence
topics.body: HOI4 loads multiple files in many folders, while some folders or filenames effectively override content. replace_path is a descriptor-level override and should be treated as high risk. Generated files should minimize conflicts by using unique IDs and mod-specific filenames.
relationships: Conflicts are often visible in error.log or by missing vanilla content.
Namespace and ID strategy reduce conflicts.
validation: Avoid writing generic filenames like 00_common.txt unless intentionally overriding.
Use mod prefixes for generated IDs.

topics.title: Paradox script structure
topics.body: HOI4 script is a key/value and nested block language. Whitespace is flexible, but readable indentation matters for review. Generators must balance braces, avoid duplicate equals signs, preserve quoted strings, and use comments only where they clarify non-obvious logic.
relationships: Triggers, effects, modifiers, and data definitions share this grammar.
GUI and GFX files use related block syntax with different keywords.
validation: Format with tabs for HOI4 script readability.
Keep generated IDs ASCII and stable.

topics.title: Scopes and scope transitions
topics.body: Scopes determine what an effect or trigger reads. ROOT, THIS, FROM, and PREV can change meaning inside nested blocks. Generated chains must explicitly understand whether they are operating on a country, state, character, unit leader, strategic region, or array element.
syntax_blocks: random_owned_state = { add_building_construction = { type = industrial_complex level = 1 instant_build = yes } }
relationships: Effects and triggers are valid only in compatible scopes.
Events and on_actions often introduce FROM or ROOT relationships.
validation: Annotate expected root scope in generated plans.
Do not nest country-only effects inside state scopes without transition.
Use temporary variables for complex scope calculations.

topics.title: Triggers and condition blocks
topics.body: Triggers are boolean conditions used in availability, visibility, AI logic, scripted triggers, decisions, focuses, events, and GUI. They should be scoped correctly and kept readable. Complex conditions should be grouped under AND, OR, NOT, limit, or scripted triggers.
relationships: Triggers gate effects; scripted_triggers reuse trigger logic; GUI triggers can compute temp variables before properties consume them.
validation: Use always = yes/no only when intentional.
Avoid expensive visible triggers in frequently refreshed GUI.

topics.title: Effects and state mutation
topics.body: Effects mutate game state. They appear in focus rewards, event options, decisions, on_actions, scripted effects, history setup, and GUI clicks. Generated effects should be minimal, scoped, and idempotent when possible.
relationships: Effects are guarded by triggers; scripted_effects share reusable effect chains; on_actions run effects in event-driven contexts.
validation: Check whether an effect is country, state, character, or unit scoped.
Avoid repeated additive effects unless the trigger prevents repetition.
Name flags and variables with mod prefixes.

topics.title: Modifiers and modifier scopes
topics.body: Modifiers alter values rather than directly running effects. They are used in ideas, traits, states, technologies, equipment, decisions, dynamic modifiers, opinion modifiers, and scripted contexts. Generated modifiers must use valid modifier keys for the target owner.
relationships: Ideas and traits wrap modifiers; dynamic modifiers are attached by effects; technologies and equipment use modifiers for gameplay values.
validation: Do not mix state-only and country-only modifiers.
Prefer small numeric values with explicit sign.
Use HOI4DOC or wiki lookup before adding unfamiliar modifier keys.

topics.title: Variables, temp variables, and math
topics.body: Variables store numbers on scopes; temp variables exist for temporary calculations. GUI, scripted effects, scripted triggers, decisions, and events can use variables for dynamic systems. Prefer explicit variable prefixes and clean temp names.
relationships: Arrays can be built from variables; GUI properties can read temp variables; MTTH and scripted localisation can reference variables.
validation: Use temp_ prefix for temp variables.

topics.title: Arrays and data structures
topics.body: Arrays store scope or value lists and enable generated dynamic content, especially scripted GUI dynamic_lists and repeated script operations. Arrays need clear owner scope and reset behavior.
relationships: Dynamic GUI lists iterate arrays; scripted effects can populate arrays; variables can be read by index.
validation: Check whether array values are scopes or numbers.

topics.title: Flags and one-time state gates
topics.body: Flags are lightweight state markers for one-time effects, event chains, GUI state, and decision unlocks. Use them for boolean state, but prefer variables when numeric state or dynamic UI frame calculation is needed.
relationships: Flags gate triggers; events set flags; decisions and focuses read flags.
validation: Avoid flags for values that should be variables.
Clear temporary flags when they represent transient state.

topics.title: Localisation encoding and key format
topics.body: HOI4 localisation yml files should be written with UTF-8 BOM. A file starts with a language root such as l_english: or l_simp_chinese:. Entries use key:0 "Text". Keep generated keys stable and match script identifiers where possible.
relationships: Script IDs usually become localisation keys; tools writing yml must preserve BOM; scripted localisation can compose dynamic text.
validation: Keep one language root per file.

topics.title: Dynamic localisation, formatting, and icons
topics.body: Localisation can reference scripted localisation, variables, icons, and formatting tokens. Generators should separate static display text from scripted dynamic branches and ensure every scripted localisation key has a fallback.
relationships: scripted_localisation.entries defines dynamic branches.
GUI textboxes and events consume localisation keys.
validation: Do not embed untranslated script IDs in visible text.

topics.title: Scripted triggers and scripted effects
topics.body: Scripted triggers and effects are reusable named blocks. They reduce duplication across events, focuses, decisions, and GUI effects. They should be written with clear scope assumptions and parameter-like naming conventions where supported by the target syntax.
relationships: Triggers return boolean; effects mutate state.
Focuses, decisions, events, and GUI can call these named blocks.
validation: Do not hide side effects in trigger-like names.

topics.title: Scripted localisation entries
topics.body: Scripted localisation chooses text by triggers. It is useful for state-dependent labels, GUI strings, event descriptions, and dynamic tooltips. Keep branch order deterministic and provide a default branch.
syntax_blocks: defined_text = { name = GetMyStatus text = { trigger = { has_war = yes } localization_key = my_status_war } text = { localization_key = my_status_peace } }
relationships: Localisation yml stores the visible keys; triggers choose which key is active.
validation: Keep branch triggers cheap for frequently refreshed GUI text.

topics.title: National focus trees
topics.body: National focus files define trees, focus nodes, prerequisites, availability, bypass logic, AI weights, and completion rewards. Generated branches need stable IDs, positions, icons, and matching localisation.
syntax_blocks: focus_tree = { id = TAG_01 country = { factor = 0 modifier = { add = 10 tag = TAG } } focus = { id = TAG_01_FS_00 x = 0 y = 0 cost = 10 completion_reward = { add_political_power = 50 } } }
relationships: Focus IDs are localisation keys and icon suffixes.
AI weights use triggers.
Rewards are effects.
validation: Use prerequisites or mutually_exclusive for branches.
Keep focus IDs unique across mods.

topics.title: Shared focuses, filters, and AI focus behavior
topics.body: Focus trees can use shared focuses, filters, bypasses, and AI weights to manage large trees. Generators should represent focus intent separately from layout so AI and branch constraints can be generated consistently.
syntax_blocks: ai_will_do = { factor = 1 modifier = { factor = 0 has_war = yes } }
relationships: AI weights consume triggers.
Shared focus definitions interact with tree-specific placement.
validation: Avoid AI factor = 0 unless intentionally disabling.
Keep filters aligned with reward type.
Do not generate impossible prerequisite chains.

topics.title: Country events
topics.body: Country events are namespaced scripted events with title, desc, trigger, immediate effects, and options. They are used for narrative chains, decision outcomes, focus rewards, and on_action responses.
syntax_blocks: country_event = { id = my_mod.1 title = my_mod.1.t desc = my_mod.1.d is_triggered_only = yes option = { name = my_mod.1.a } }
relationships: Options run effects; localisation keys provide text; on_actions and focuses fire events.
validation: Avoid missing is_triggered_only when event should not pulse.

topics.title: News events and MTTH
topics.body: News events target global presentation while MTTH-driven events evaluate over time. Generated events must avoid accidental spam and must distinguish triggered-only chains from recurring pulse logic.
syntax_blocks: news_event = { id = my_mod.10 title = my_mod.10.t desc = my_mod.10.d major = yes }
mean_time_to_happen = { days = 30 modifier = { factor = 0 has_war = no } }
relationships: MTTH uses triggers and modifiers.
News events need localisation and pictures.

topics.title: Event chains and delayed follow-up
topics.body: Event chains pass context through scopes and delayed event calls. Generators need to preserve ROOT/FROM expectations and avoid losing the original actor when chaining through states or characters.
relationships: Scope handling controls who receives follow-up events.
Flags often prevent duplicate branches.
validation: Set flags before delayed events if repetition matters.

topics.title: On actions and pulse hooks
topics.body: On actions attach events or effects to game hooks such as startup, war, peace, daily, weekly, and monthly pulses. They are powerful and can become performance hazards if generated without tight triggers.
relationships: On actions fire events and scripted effects.
Pulse hooks must use cheap triggers.
validation: Avoid heavy logic directly in frequent pulses.
Do not add global hooks without a clear mod prefix.

topics.title: Decision categories and decisions
topics.body: Decision categories group decisions; individual decisions control visibility, availability, cost, timers, removal, effects, and AI use. Generated decisions must distinguish visible from available so unavailable choices can still be intentionally shown.
syntax_blocks: my_category = { icon = generic_decisions my_decision = { icon = generic_decision cost = 25 available = { always = yes } complete_effect = { add_political_power = -25 } } }
relationships: Decisions consume triggers and effects.
Localisation keys usually match category and decision IDs.
validation: Use visible for display gates and available for click gates.
Set fire_only_once or days_remove where relevant.

topics.title: Missions, timers, and recurring decisions
topics.body: Timed decisions and missions run after a duration or disappear after conditions change. They need clear timeout, cancellation, and repeat semantics so generated content does not loop or disappear unexpectedly.
relationships: Timed decisions often drive event chains.
AI behavior controls repeated use.
validation: Use flags or variables for chain state.

topics.title: Ideas, advisors, and national spirits
topics.body: Idea files define national spirits, advisors, manufacturers, theorists, and other idea types. The key content is picture, allowed/visible/available logic, cost, traits, and modifier blocks.
syntax_blocks: ideas = { country = { my_idea = { picture = my_idea modifier = { stability_factor = 0.05 } } } }
relationships: Ideas wrap modifiers.
Characters can own advisor roles.
Localisation keys match idea IDs.
validation: Do not mix incompatible advisor fields.

topics.title: Dynamic modifiers and scripted value-driven buffs
topics.body: Dynamic modifiers let script attach a reusable modifier block to a country or state. They are useful for systems where effects add, remove, or update persistent buffs without creating many ideas.
relationships: Effects attach dynamic modifiers.
Variables can drive scripted values related to modifiers.
validation: Remove old dynamic modifiers before reapplying when necessary.

topics.title: Characters, leaders, advisors, and roles
topics.body: Characters define political leaders, advisors, field marshals, corps commanders, navy leaders, and other roles. Generated characters need portraits, traits, ideology, country ownership, and recruitment or history integration.
syntax_blocks: characters = { my_character = { name = my_character_name portraits = { civilian = { large = GFX_portrait_my_character } } country_leader = { ideology = neutrality traits = { } } } }
relationships: Ideas can reference character advisors.
History files recruit or set leaders.
Traits provide modifiers.

topics.title: Leader and advisor traits
topics.body: Traits define modifiers and constraints for leaders, generals, admirals, and advisors. Generated traits should be tied to the correct character role and should not reuse vanilla IDs unless intentionally modifying them.
relationships: Traits are assigned inside character role blocks.
Trait modifiers share modifier scope rules.

topics.title: Country tags and country creation
topics.body: Country creation involves tag registration, country history, color, graphical culture, flags, localisation, and optionally cosmetic tags. Generated content should treat the three-letter tag as a namespace root.
relationships: History, focus trees, ideas, and localisation all reference tags.
Cosmetic tags alter display identity.
validation: Add localisation for country and adjective.

topics.title: Cosmetic tags and dynamic country names
topics.body: Cosmetic tags change a country's visible name, flag, and identity without changing its underlying tag. They are useful for government changes, civil war outcomes, and scripted narrative states.
relationships: Cosmetic tags require localisation and flags.
Events and focuses commonly set cosmetic tags.
validation: Do not treat cosmetic tag as a real country tag in history files.

topics.title: Country history setup
topics.body: Country history files set starting politics, capital, technology, OOB references, characters, ideas, and starting effects. Generated setup must be deterministic and compatible with the selected start date.
relationships: Country history references states, OOB files, characters, ideas, and technologies.

topics.title: State history, ownership, cores, and buildings
topics.body: State history defines owner, controller, cores, claims, buildings, victory points, resources, and province-level history. Generated state edits are conflict-prone and should be scoped to explicit state IDs.
syntax_blocks: history = { owner = TAG add_core_of = TAG buildings = { infrastructure = 3 arms_factory = 2 } victory_points = { 1234 10 } }
relationships: Country history needs valid capital state.
Map and strategic regions contextualize state IDs.
validation: Avoid replacing whole state files unless intended.
Keep victory point province IDs valid.

topics.title: OOB files and starting units
topics.body: Order of battle files define starting divisions, ships, air wings, and templates. Generated OOB must reference valid province IDs, equipment, division templates, and technology availability.
syntax_blocks: division = { name = "1st Division" location = 1234 division_template = "Infantry Division" start_experience_factor = 0.1 }
relationships: Country history loads OOB.
Division templates and equipment archetypes must exist.
validation: Avoid giving equipment that does not exist.

topics.title: Map provinces, terrain, and continent data
topics.body: Map modding covers province definitions, terrain, continents, heightmaps, rivers, and related files. It is a high-risk domain because visual files and CSV data must agree exactly.
relationships: States reference provinces.
Strategic regions and supply systems rely on map data.
validation: Do not generate map edits without explicit user confirmation.
Validate CSV and bitmap consistency externally.

topics.title: Adjacencies, strategic regions, and supply map
topics.body: Adjacencies define special map connections; strategic regions group provinces for air/naval logic; supply nodes and railways define logistics. Generated content must cross-check province IDs and region membership.
relationships: Air regions and naval regions affect AI and combat.
State edits interact with supply and strategic regions.
validation: Do not infer supply topology from state ownership alone.

topics.title: Technology trees and research
topics.body: Technology files define researchable items, folders, paths, prerequisites, bonuses, and equipment unlocks. Generated techs must integrate with folders, icons, localisation, and equipment or unit systems.
syntax_blocks: technologies = { my_tech = { area = industry start_year = 1936 cost = 1.0 } }
relationships: Technologies unlock equipment and modify units.
Focuses and decisions can grant research bonuses.

topics.title: Equipment archetypes and variants
topics.body: Equipment files define archetypes, concrete equipment, stats, build cost, year, parent relationships, and interface category. Generated equipment must be unlocked by tech or history and referenced by units or OOB.
syntax_blocks: equipments = { my_equipment = { year = 1936 archetype = infantry_equipment build_cost_ic = 0.5 } }
relationships: Technologies unlock equipment.
OOB and units consume equipment.
GFX and localisation expose equipment to UI.

topics.title: Division templates and land units
topics.body: Division templates define regiment layout, support companies, priority, and displayed names. Generated templates must match available subunits and equipment, and OOB divisions must reference exact template names.
syntax_blocks: division_template = { name = "Infantry Division" regiments = { infantry = { x = 0 y = 0 } } support = { engineer = { x = 0 y = 0 } } }
relationships: OOB divisions use templates.
Subunits define equipment and stats.
Technologies unlock battalions.

topics.title: Sub-units, battalions, and support companies
topics.body: Sub-units define battalion and support company stats, categories, equipment needs, map icons, and combat behavior. Generated subunits are gameplay-balance sensitive and require technology and equipment integration.
syntax_blocks: sub_units = { my_battalion = { sprite = infantry type = infantry group = infantry combat_width = 2 } }
relationships: Division templates reference subunits.
Equipment and technology gate availability.
validation: Test combat width and AI usage.

topics.title: Buildings, resources, and state modifiers
topics.body: Buildings and resources are commonly set in state history or modified by effects. Generated economy content should distinguish permanent state setup from scripted construction and temporary modifiers.
relationships: State history sets initial values.
Effects can add construction or slots.
Modifiers alter output factors.
validation: Avoid state file replacement unless intended.

topics.title: GUI layout and GFX sprite registration
topics.body: GUI layout files define windows and controls; GFX files register sprites and images. Generated GUI must keep element names, scripted_gui references, GFX names, and asset paths aligned.
relationships: Scripted GUI triggers/effects/properties reference GUI element names.
Localisation provides text for textboxes.

topics.title: Scripted GUI dynamic lists and properties
topics.body: Scripted GUI binds GUI windows to script-driven visibility, click effects, dynamic properties, and dynamic lists. It is the core bridge for custom HOI4 interfaces and must be generated with exact element names.
syntax_blocks: scripted_gui = { my_gui = { context_type = player_context window_name = "my_window" visible = { always = yes } dynamic_lists = { my_grid = { array = ROOT.my_array entry_container = my_entry change_scope = no value = my_value index = my_index } } } }
relationships: GUI element names must match triggers, effects, and properties.
Arrays and temp variables drive dynamic lists and positions.
GFX registers images used by properties.
validation: Clear and rebuild temp arrays before dynamic list calculations.

topics.title: Interface assets, fonts, and sprite sheets
topics.body: Interface assets include textures, fonts, sprite sheets, and definitions for GUI controls. Generated asset references should be stable and mod-local to avoid collisions with vanilla interface files.
relationships: GUI controls consume GFX sprites.
Localisation text must fit textboxes.
Asset dimensions influence layout.

topics.title: AI strategies and scripted AI behavior
topics.body: AI strategy files and ai_will_do blocks steer how AI countries pick focuses, decisions, diplomacy, production, and plans. Generated AI logic should be conservative and debuggable.
syntax_blocks: ai_will_do = { factor = 1 modifier = { factor = 0 has_war = yes } }
ai_strategy = { type = conquer id = TAG value = 50 }
relationships: Focuses and decisions use ai_will_do.
Country AI strategies interact with diplomacy and war goals.
validation: Gate AI behavior by tag or situation.

topics.title: Diplomacy, opinion, factions, and autonomy
topics.body: Diplomacy scripting covers opinions, factions, guarantees, war goals, autonomy state, subjects, and scripted diplomatic effects. Generated content must distinguish target and actor scopes.
relationships: Opinion modifiers need definitions and localisation.
War goals and factions often come from events or focuses.
validation: Localise opinion modifiers and faction names.

topics.title: Game rules and startup configuration
topics.body: Game rules expose startup choices that can alter country setup, AI behavior, or mod systems. Generated game rules need clear localisation, default choices, and script hooks that read the selected option.
syntax_blocks: game_rule = { name = my_rule group = my_group option = { name = default } }
relationships: Startup events and on_actions read game rules.
Localisation names rule groups and options.
validation: Do not require DLC-specific behavior unless documented.
Use rule checks in generated triggers.

topics.title: Defines and game constants
topics.body: Defines override game constants and affect broad systems. They are powerful but risky for compatibility. Generated mods should avoid defines unless the user explicitly requests balance or engine-level tuning.
relationships: Defines can affect every generated content system.
Latest patch snapshots matter for define compatibility.
validation: Do not generate defines by default.
Verify define names against current game version.

topics.title: Bookmarks and start dates
topics.body: Bookmarks define scenario selection, highlighted countries, start dates, and visible descriptions. Generated bookmarks must match history setup and localisation.
syntax_blocks: bookmarks = { bookmark = { date = 1936.1.1 default = yes selectable = { TAG } } }
relationships: Country and state history may contain date-specific blocks.
Bookmarks depend on country tags and localisation.
validation: Ensure start date has matching history.

topics.title: Sound, music, and asset registration
topics.body: Audio modding registers music, sound effects, and asset files. Generated references should keep file paths relative, include required metadata, and avoid replacing vanilla audio unintentionally.
relationships: Events and GUI can trigger sounds.
Music stations need localisation and asset files.

topics.title: Common generation and loading errors
topics.body: Common generated-file failures include missing braces, extra equals signs, wrong folders, missing localisation BOM, mismatched GFX names, invalid scope assumptions, duplicate IDs, missing assets, and hidden vanilla content caused by replace_path.
relationships: Every generator should surface path, ID, encoding, and scope assumptions.
Error logs should guide targeted fixes instead of blind regeneration.
validation: Run game with debug logs for release-critical content.
Check localisation and GFX names when UI text or sprites are missing.
